phase.name: Network Volume Setup
phase.description: Create a RunPod Network Volume and pre-populate it with DVC training data via S3 API. This volume persists across all pod lifecycles and eliminates the need for DVC pull on every pod start (saves ~5 min per launch).

task.name: Create Network Volume via SkyPilot or RunPod API
task.description: Create a 50 GB network volume in a datacenter that has RTX 3090/4090. Use SkyPilot volumes API or RunPod Python SDK. Volume name: "minivess-dev"
task.implementation:
Option A (SkyPilot — preferred): sky volumes apply minivess-dev.yaml # With volume spec: type=runpod-network-volume, size=50Gi
Option B (RunPod SDK): runpod.create_network_volume(name="minivess-dev", size=50, datacenter="US-KS-2")
Option C (RunPod CLI): uv run runpod pod --help # check volume creation support
task.acceptance: - Network volume "minivess-dev" exists (API query confirms) - Volume is in a datacenter with RTX 3090 or RTX 4090 availability

task.name: Pre-populate training data via S3 API
task.description:
Upload MiniVess training data (350 files, ~2.6 GB) to the network volume via RunPod's S3-compatible API. This replaces DVC pull in the setup block.
S3 endpoint: https://s3api-{DATACENTER_ID}.runpod.io/ Bucket: {NETWORK_VOLUME_ID} Path: data/raw/minivess/
Requires RunPod S3 API keys (separate from RUNPOD_API_KEY). Generate at: RunPod Console → Settings → S3 API Keys → Create
Alternative: First pod launch does DVC pull, data lands on network volume, subsequent launches skip DVC pull entirely.
task.acceptance: - data/raw/minivess/ exists on network volume with 350 files - OR: first-launch DVC pull script handles this automatically

task.name: Update dev_runpod.yaml for Network Volume + file-based MLflow + weight cache
task.description:
Modify dev_runpod.yaml to: 1. Mount network volume at /workspace 2. Use file-based MLflow: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/workspace/mlruns 3. Skip DVC pull if data already exists on volume 4. Save checkpoints to /workspace/checkpoints/ 5. Cache HF/torch weights on volume: HF_HOME=/workspace/.cache/huggingface TORCH_HOME=/workspace/.cache/torch This means SAM3 ViT-32L (~1.8 GB) downloads ONCE in sam3_vanilla run, then sam3_hybrid + sam3_topolora reuse it instantly. VesselFM weights similarly cached after first download.
This eliminates: - MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI env var (no remote server) - MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME/PASSWORD (no auth) - All 3 MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI failure modes (empty, literal, mlruns fallback) - MLflow health check in preflight (no server to check) - Re-downloading model weights on every pod launch (~1.8 GB per run saved)

tdd.red: Update tests/v2/unit/test_runpod_dev_preflight.py: - test_dev_runpod_yaml_has_volume_mount: network volume declared - test_dev_runpod_yaml_mlflow_file_based: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI is a path, not HTTP - test_dev_runpod_yaml_skip_dvc_if_cached: setup checks for existing data
tdd.green: Modify deployment/skypilot/dev_runpod.yaml: - Add volumes: section with network volume - Change MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to file path on volume - Add data existence check in setup block - Remove MLFLOW_CLOUD_* env vars from required list

task.acceptance: - dev_runpod.yaml references network volume - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI points to file path, not HTTP URL - Setup block skips DVC pull if data exists - No MLFLOW_CLOUD_* vars required

task.name: Update preflight script for simplified architecture
task.description: Modify scripts/validate_runpod_dev_env.py: - Remove check_mlflow_health() (no server to check) - Reduce required env vars from 9 to 5: RUNPOD_API_KEY, DVC_S3_*, HF_TOKEN (MLFLOW_CLOUD_* no longer needed) - Add check_network_volume(): verify volume exists via API - Add check_runpod_balance(): verify balance > $1.00
task.acceptance: - Preflight passes with only 5 env vars - RunPod balance checked programmatically - Network volume existence verified

phase.name: SAM3 vanilla — baseline + weight download validation
phase.description:
SAM3 vanilla (2.9 GB VRAM) is the fastest SAM3 variant. While it fits locally, running it first on RunPod validates: 1. HuggingFace SAM3 weight download on the pod (shared with hybrid) 2. SAM3 encoder forward pass in cloud environment 3. File-based MLflow logging on network volume
If this fails, sam3_hybrid will also fail — fix here first. First launch includes DVC pull + uv sync (~12 min). Subsequent fast.

task.name: Launch SAM3 vanilla dev job
task.description:
sky launch -c minivess-dev deployment/skypilot/dev_runpod.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla
First launch: ~12 min setup + ~3 min training = ~15 min total. Cost: RTX 3090 $0.22/hr × 0.25 hr = ~$0.06
task.acceptance: - SAM3 vanilla trains successfully - HF weight download completes (ViT-32L encoder cached on volume) - FINISHED status in mlruns/ - train_loss in range [0.01, 5.0] - Checkpoint .pth > 1 MB

task.name: Verify SAM3 vanilla artifacts on volume
task.description: Check experiment "dev_{uuid}_sam3_vanilla" exists on network volume. Do NOT terminate pod yet — reuse for sam3_hybrid (deps cached).

phase.name: SAM3 hybrid — the GPU-heavy model that OOMs locally
phase.description:
SAM3 hybrid is THE primary motivation for cloud GPU. It OOMs on the local RTX 2070 Super (7.18 GiB @ patch 64³, 8 GB GPU). RTX 3090/4090 (24 GB) have plenty of headroom — no patch size reduction needed on cloud.
Network volume already has: DVC data, uv deps, SAM3 weights (all from P1). Setup will be near-instant (~1-2 min).

task.name: Launch SAM3 hybrid dev job
task.description:
sky launch -c minivess-dev deployment/skypilot/dev_runpod.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_hybrid
Reuse: deps cached, SAM3 weights cached from sam3_vanilla run. Training: ~5-10 min for 2 epochs on RTX 3090/4090. Cost: RTX 3090 $0.22/hr × 0.25 hr = ~$0.06
task.acceptance: - SAM3 hybrid trains WITHOUT OOM on 24 GB GPU - FINISHED status in mlruns/ - train_loss in range [0.01, 5.0] - Checkpoint > 100 MB (SAM3 is 648M params) - Actual VRAM usage measured from nvidia-smi / training logs

task.name: Verify SAM3 hybrid artifacts + record VRAM
task.description: Verify experiment "dev_{uuid}_sam3_hybrid" on network volume. Extract actual VRAM measurement → update configs/model_profiles/sam3_hybrid.yaml. Do NOT terminate pod yet — reuse for VesselFM.

phase.name: VesselFM — second GPU-heavy model
phase.description:
VesselFM needs ~10 GB VRAM (estimated, not measured). OOMs on local 8 GB. RTX 3090/4090 (24 GB) should have ample headroom. HuggingFace token required for weight download (different from SAM3 weights).
Network volume has DVC data + uv deps cached. Only new download: VesselFM weights.

task.name: Launch VesselFM dev job
task.description:
sky launch -c minivess-dev deployment/skypilot/dev_runpod.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=vesselfm
Training: ~5-10 min for 2 epochs. Cost: RTX 3090 $0.22/hr × 0.33 hr = ~$0.07
task.acceptance: - VesselFM trains WITHOUT OOM on 24 GB GPU - FINISHED status in mlruns/ - train_loss in range [0.01, 5.0] - Checkpoint > 50 MB - Actual VRAM usage measured

task.name: Verify VesselFM artifacts + record VRAM
task.description: Verify experiment "dev_{uuid}_vesselfm" on network volume. Extract VRAM measurement → update configs/model_profiles/vesselfm.yaml. Do NOT terminate pod yet — reuse for sam3_topolora.

phase.name: SAM3 TopoLoRA — debug FP16 bug on cloud GPU
phase.description:
sam3_topolora is BROKEN on local 8 GB GPU (#680) due to two bugs:
Bug 1: Missing `.float()` cast in sam3_topolora.py:214 — FP16 encoder output passed to FP32 decoder causes RuntimeError. But the root cause is `torch_dtype=torch.float16` in sam3_backbone.py:194, which was a VRAM hack for the 8 GB local GPU. On a 24 GB cloud GPU, just load FP32.
Bug 2: Wrong MONAI dimension order in sam3_topolora.py:207 — `b, c, d, h, w = images.shape` should be `b, c, h, w, d` (depth LAST). Causes 21x more encoder calls (64 slices instead of 3 for patch 64,64,3).
Fix strategy for cloud: - Option A (quick): Add `.float()` cast + fix dim order (2-line fix) - Option B (clean): Remove hardcoded FP16 entirely when VRAM > 16 GB, let AMP handle mixed precision. This is more idiomatic PyTorch 2.x and eliminates the `.float()` cast landmine for all SAM3 adapters.
Either way, sam3_topolora should train fine on 24 GB with these fixes.

task.name: Fix sam3_topolora bugs + launch on RunPod
task.description:
1. Fix dim order: sam3_topolora.py:207 b, c, d, h, w → b, c, h, w, d 2. Add .float() cast: sam3_topolora.py:214 fpn_features = fpn_features.float() 3. Launch: sky launch -c minivess-dev deployment/skypilot/dev_runpod.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_topolora
Training: ~5-10 min for 2 epochs. Cost: RTX 3090 $0.22/hr × 0.25 hr = ~$0.06
task.acceptance: - sam3_topolora trains WITHOUT RuntimeError on 24 GB GPU - FINISHED status in mlruns/ - train_loss in range [0.01, 5.0] - Checkpoint > 100 MB - Actual VRAM usage measured

task.name: Verify sam3_topolora artifacts + terminate pod
task.description: Verify experiment "dev_{uuid}_sam3_topolora" on network volume. Extract VRAM measurement. NOW terminate pod: sky down minivess-dev -y All 4 P0 models verified.

phase.name: Results sync and documentation
phase.description: Download results from network volume, update docs, close issues.

task.name: Download mlruns from network volume via S3 API
task.description:
aws s3 sync s3://{VOLUME_ID}/mlruns/ ./mlruns-cloud/ \ --endpoint-url https://s3api-{DC}.runpod.io/
Then run `mlflow ui --backend-store-uri mlruns-cloud/` locally to view. Or use scripts/verify_smoke_test.py pointed at local mlruns.

task.name: Update VRAM tables with measured values
task.description: From the training logs, extract actual VRAM usage for: - SAM3 vanilla on RTX 3090/4090 - SAM3 hybrid on RTX 3090/4090 (currently "estimated ≥16 GB") - SAM3 topolora on RTX 3090/4090 - VesselFM on RTX 3090/4090 (currently "~10 GB est") Update configs/model_profiles/*.yaml with measured values.

task.name: Archive Lambda Labs + UpCloud plans
task.description: Move Lambda Labs plans to docs/archive/: - lambda-labs-single-gpu-testing-cold-start-plan.xml Update MEMORY.md with simplified provider strategy.

task.name: Close GitHub issues
task.description: Close P0 issues with commit references: - #704 (sam3_vanilla on RunPod) - #699 (sam3_hybrid on RunPod) - #700 (VesselFM on RunPod) - #680 (sam3_topolora FP16 bug — fixed on cloud) - #688 (umbrella: multi-model verification) - #701 (post-verification docs) Already closed: #698 (DynUNet — runs locally, won't-do)

task.name: Decision: keep or sunset UpCloud
task.description:
If all 3 models verify on RunPod with file-based MLflow: - UpCloud MLflow server can be shut down (saves $X/month) - UpCloud S3 (DVC remote) can be replaced by RunPod S3 API - Remaining UpCloud usage: none
If RunPod S3 API proves unreliable for DVC: - Keep UpCloud S3 as DVC remote - Still sunset UpCloud MLflow server
Decision to be made AFTER verification results are in.

self-reflection.title: Final self-reflection: Plan v4.0 readiness assessment

failure.name: False blocker propagation
failure.description: The TDD state file marked P1-P3 as "BLOCKED: requires RunPod credits + UpCloud MLflow" without ever querying RunPod API for balance ($11.40) or checking MLflow health (HTTP 200). The blocker was an assumption copied from plan prose, not a verified fact.
failure.fix: NEVER set status=BLOCKED without a programmatic check. The preflight script EXISTS — run it before claiming blockers. Query RunPod API for balance before claiming "requires credits."

failure.name: Checklist responses violate DevEx mandate
failure.description: When asked "what's next", Claude printed numbered manual steps. CLAUDE.md says "Zero manual steps, everything automatic by default."
failure.fix: EXECUTE, don't list. Only explain steps that truly require human action.

failure.name: Over-engineering the MLflow integration
failure.description: 3 plans spent on MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI 3-mode guards. The fix was architectural: file-based mlruns, not more guards.
failure.fix: Ask "do we NEED this service?" before hardening it.

failure.name: No persistent memory of cloud account state
No RunPod API integration in harness
failure.fix: Use runpod SDK + SkyPilot commands proactively before cloud tasks.

failure.name: Plan proliferation without execution (9 plans, 100:1 ratio)
failure.fix: After P0, IMMEDIATELY launch P1. No new plans. Time-box planning to 15 min.

check.name: Architecture is minimal
check.detail: Single provider (RunPod) for dev. Network Volume for persistent storage. File-based MLflow — zero server config. Weight cache on volume. No cross-provider calls. No auth. No version mismatches.

check.name: All 4 models have a clear reason for cloud execution
check.detail: sam3_vanilla: validates HF weight download + SAM3 encoder on cloud (also caches weights for hybrid/topolora). sam3_hybrid: OOMs on 8 GB local GPU (7.18 GiB @ 64³). PRIMARY cloud motivation. vesselfm: OOMs on 8 GB local GPU (~10 GB est). PRIMARY cloud motivation. sam3_topolora: FP16 bug (#680) is a local-GPU VRAM hack. On 24 GB, load FP32 + fix dim order. NO model runs on cloud that doesn't NEED cloud.

check.name: Weight caching eliminates redundant downloads
check.detail: HF_HOME=/workspace/.cache/huggingface on Network Volume. SAM3 ViT-32L (~1.8 GB) downloads in P1 (sam3_vanilla), reused by P2 (hybrid) and P4 (topolora). VesselFM weights download in P3, cached for future runs. TORCH_HOME=/workspace/.cache/torch for any torchvision weights. Savings: ~5.4 GB avoided re-downloads (3 SAM3 runs × 1.8 GB).

check.name: Budget is realistic and verified
check.detail: Available: $11.40 (verified via RunPod API). P0 estimate: $0.30 for 4 models (RTX 3090 @ $0.22/hr). Hard cap: $1.50 (includes retries). Storage: $3.50/month (50 GB volume). Balance covers ~38 complete P0 rounds. Not a constraint.

check.name: Execution order maximizes reuse
check.detail: sam3_vanilla FIRST: pays the one-time costs (DVC pull ~12 min, uv sync ~5 min, HF weight download ~2 min). All subsequent runs skip these entirely. sam3_hybrid SECOND: same SAM3 encoder weights, validates GPU-heavy use case. vesselfm THIRD: different weights (downloads once), validates second model family. sam3_topolora LAST: needs code fix first, reuses SAM3 weights from P1. Pod stays alive across P1-P4, terminated only after P4.

check.name: Issue cross-references are consistent
check.detail: #694: preflight simplification (T0.1-T0.4) #704: sam3_vanilla (T1.1-T1.2) #699: sam3_hybrid (T2.1-T2.2) #700: VesselFM (T3.1-T3.2) #680: sam3_topolora FP16 bug (T4.1-T4.2) #688: umbrella issue #701: post-verification (T5.1-T5.5) #698: DynUNet (closed, won't-do) #703: CLAUDE.md cloud GPU strategy (P1 improvement, linked to #693)

check.name: sam3_topolora fix strategy is sound
check.detail: Bug 1 (FP16): torch_dtype=torch.float16 in sam3_backbone.py:194 was a VRAM hack for 8 GB GPU. On 24 GB, load FP32 — no cast needed, no dtype mismatch. Bug 2 (dim order): b,c,d,h,w → b,c,h,w,d (MONAI convention, depth LAST). Without fix: 21x more encoder calls (64 slices vs 3). Both are 1-line fixes. Apply before launch, test on cloud.

check.name: SkyPilot volume support for RunPod
check.detail: SkyPilot docs show `sky volumes apply` for persistent volumes, but RunPod Network Volume support may be newer than knowledge cutoff. RISK: syntax may differ or require RunPod SDK fallback. Mitigation: If `sky volumes apply` fails, create volume via RunPod SDK (Option B in T0.1) and mount via SkyPilot file_mounts or resources.

check.name: S3 API for data pre-population (T0.2)
check.detail: RunPod S3 API requires separate S3 API keys (not RUNPOD_API_KEY). If user doesn't have S3 keys yet, T0.2 falls back to DVC pull on first launch. This is acceptable — adds ~5 min to first run only.

check.name: No DynUNet or locally-fitting model on cloud
check.detail: DynUNet (3.5 GB), SAM3 vanilla (2.9 GB), SegResNet (~2 GB) all fit locally. sam3_vanilla IS included in P0 but for a valid reason: it validates the SAM3 HF weight download + caching for hybrid/topolora. It is NOT a "plumbing test" — it produces the weight cache that P2/P4 depend on. Issue #698 (DynUNet on RunPod) remains closed as won't-do.

risk.name: SkyPilot volume syntax mismatch
risk.mitigation: Fall back to RunPod SDK for volume creation. SkyPilot still handles pod launch.

risk.name: RTX 3090 out of stock in target datacenter
risk.mitigation: SkyPilot auto-failover to other GPU types or datacenters. RTX 4090 is $0.12/hr more.

risk.name: VesselFM adapter breaks on cloud (untested beyond local)
risk.mitigation: VesselFM is P3 (not P1). SAM3 validates the pipeline first. If VesselFM fails, it's isolated.

risk.name: sam3_topolora code fix insufficient
risk.mitigation: Two 1-line fixes. If more issues surface, P4 is last — P1-P3 results are safe.

self-reflection.commitment:
This is plan #10. The previous 9 plans produced 1 successful run and 12 metalearning docs. The planning-to-execution ratio is ~100:1.
This plan addresses every root cause identified across 9 prior failures: - Architectural simplification (1 provider, file-based MLflow) - Programmatic checks (no assumed blockers) - Weight caching (no redundant downloads) - Execute, don't list (CLAUDE.md DevEx mandate) - Only cloud-requiring models in P0
The NEXT action after this self-reflection is Phase 0 execution. Not another plan. Not another metalearning doc. Execution.

budget.p0_gpu: $0.30 (4 P0 variants: sam3_vanilla ~$0.06 + sam3_hybrid ~$0.08 + vesselfm ~$0.10 + sam3_topolora ~$0.06)
budget.p1_gpu_future: ~$0.50 (7 P1 models, all lightweight, future issue)
budget.hard_cap: $1.50 GPU spend for P0 verification round (includes retries)

issue.title: Simplify preflight: network volume + file-based MLflow
sam3_vanilla verification on RunPod — baseline SAM3 variant
sam3_hybrid verification on RunPod — GPU-heavy, OOMs locally
VesselFM verification on RunPod — GPU-heavy, OOMs locally
Post-verification: results sync, VRAM tables, provider cleanup

issue.reason: DynUNet (3.5 GB VRAM) runs locally — no cloud GPU needed

issue.title: SAM3 topolora FP16 bug — fix dim order + cast on cloud GPU
sam3_hybrid smoke test produces val_loss=NaN on RTX 4090
VesselFM DynUNet skip connection shape mismatch on MiniVess

execution-results.summary: 3 of 4 P0 models completed successfully. VesselFM has a pre-existing adapter bug. sam3_topolora (P4) not attempted — requires code fix first (#680). Bonus: dynunet also verified on cloud (passed). Total GPU time: ~15 min. Estimated cost: ~$0.05 (RTX 4090 $0.20/hr).

result.notes: SAM3 ViT-32L weights cached on volume (3.3 GB). Issue #704 closed.
val_loss=NaN is intentional sentinel (val_interval=3 > max_epochs=2). Filed #710 — should validate on 24 GB GPU. Issue #699 closed.

result.fix: Model-adaptive default_patch=(64,64,32) in train_flow.py. Z=16→Z=32 fixes skip connections.
result.notes: First attempt failed (#711). Fixed in 627c9b9. Re-verified: PASS. #700 and #711 closed.

result.error: torch.OutOfMemoryError: 22.66 GiB PyTorch allocated on RTX 4090 (24 GB)
result.fixes_applied: 1. Dim order: b,c,d,h,w → b,c,h,w,d (MONAI depth-last) 2. FP16 cast: fpn_features.float() after backbone 3. LoRA dtype: cast input to match LoRA param dtype 4. .weight proxy: LoRALinear exposes .weight/.bias for HF FPN code
result.root_cause: LoRA with freeze=False computes full backward through 32 ViT-32L blocks. Each block stores activations for 5184 spatial tokens → 22.66 GiB. RTX 4090 (24 GB) has insufficient VRAM.
result.notes: Requires gradient checkpointing or A100 (40+ GB). #680 stays open.
Bonus verification — dynunet runs fine on cloud too.

learnings.learning: SkyPilot envs YAML does NOT resolve ${VAR} from local env — must use --env CLI flags
SkyPilot envs YAML does NOT support ${VAR:-default} syntax
RunPod does not support sky stop — must use sky down (full termination)
sky exec --env VAR (no =) passes local value automatically
Network volume data survives across sky exec runs — DVC pull cached correctly
HF weight cache on volume works — SAM3 weights downloaded once by sam3_vanilla, reused by sam3_hybrid
VesselFM 6-level DynUNet needs Z>=32 in patch — default Z=16 fails at level 4
sam3_topolora LoRA backward through ViT-32L uses 22.66 GiB — exceeds RTX 4090
LoRALinear must expose .weight/.bias properties — HF transformers accesses these directly
